testcase.name: test_compute_occupancy_df
testcase.failure:
def test_compute_occupancy_df():
data_dict = {'time' : np.array([5, 5, 5, 5, 5, 5, 5, 5, 5.]), 'xbin' : np.array([0, 0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 2, 2])}
# check 1d case bins = 3 bindf = pd.DataFrame(data_dict) occ = compute_occupancy_df(bindf, bins) assert isinstance(occ, np.ndarray) assert np.array_equal(occ, np.array([20, 15, 10]))
# check 2d case data_dict['ybin'] = np.array([0, 0, 1, 1, 0, 1, 1, 0, 1]) bins = [3, 2] bindf = pd.DataFrame(data_dict) occ = compute_occupancy_df(bindf, bins) assert isinstance(occ, np.ndarray) assert np.array_equal(occ.shape, np.array([bins[1], bins[0]])) assert np.array_equal(occ, np.array([[10, 5, 5], [10, 10, 5]]))
# check minimum > occ = compute_occupancy_df(bindf, bins, minimum=6) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
spiketools/tests/spatial/test_occupancy.py:202: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
bindf = time xbin ybin 0 5.0 0 0 1 5.0 0 0 2 5.0 0 1 3 5.0 0 1 4 5.0 1 0 5 5.0 1 1 6 5.0 1 1 7 5.0 2 0 8 5.0 2 1 bins = [3, 2], minimum = 6, normalize = False, set_nan = False
def compute_occupancy_df(bindf, bins, minimum=None, normalize=False, set_nan=False): """Compute the bin occupancy from bin-position dataframe.
Parameters ---------- bindf : pd.DataFrame Dataframe representation of position bin information. bins : int or list of [int, int] The bin definition for dividing up the space. If 1d, can be integer. If 2d should be a list, defined as [number of x_bins, number of y_bins]. minimum : float, optional The minimum required occupancy. If defined, any values below this are set to zero. normalize : bool, optional, default: False Whether to normalize occupancy to sum to 1. set_nan : bool, optional, default: False Whether to set zero occupancy locations as NaN.
Returns ------- occupancy : 1d or 2d array Computed occupancy across the space. For 2d, has shape [n_y_bins, n_x_bins] (see notes in `compute_occupancy`). """
bins = check_bin_definition(bins)
# Group position samples into spatial bins, summing total time spent there groupby = sorted([el for el in list(bindf.columns) if 'bin' in el]) bingroup = bindf.groupby(groupby)['time'].sum() # This standardizes any unobserved values to be zero # This can otherwise vary by code version (sometimes being NaN instead) bingroup = bingroup.fillna(0)
# Extract and re-organize occupancy into array occupancy = bingroup.values.reshape(*bins) occupancy = occupancy.T
if minimum: > occupancy[occupancy < minimum] = 0. ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E ValueError: assignment destination is read-only
spiketools/spatial/occupancy.py:423: ValueError

testcase.name: test_compute_occupancy

failure.message: ValueError: cannot reshape array of size 3 into shape (2,3)

testcase.failure:
def test_compute_occupancy():
# Test 1d case bins = 3 position = np.array([1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 9, 10]) timestamps = np.linspace(0, 30, len(position)) occ = compute_occupancy(position, timestamps, bins) expected1 = np.array([15, 10, 5]) assert isinstance(occ, np.ndarray) assert occ.shape[0] == bins assert np.array_equal(occ, expected1)
# Test 2d case bins = [2, 3] position = np.array([[1, 2, 3, 4, 4.5, 5], [6, 7, 8, 8.5, 9.5, 10]]) timestamps = np.linspace(0, 25, position.shape[1]) > occ = compute_occupancy(position, timestamps, bins) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
spiketools/tests/spatial/test_occupancy.py:231: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ spiketools/spatial/occupancy.py:503: in compute_occupancy occupancy = compute_occupancy_df(df, bins, minimum, normalize, set_nan) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
bindf = time xbin ybin 0 5.0 0 0 1 5.0 0 0 2 5.0 1 1 3 5.0 1 1 4 5.0 1 2 5 0.0 1 2 bins = [2, 3], minimum = None, normalize = False, set_nan = False
def compute_occupancy_df(bindf, bins, minimum=None, normalize=False, set_nan=False): """Compute the bin occupancy from bin-position dataframe.
Parameters ---------- bindf : pd.DataFrame Dataframe representation of position bin information. bins : int or list of [int, int] The bin definition for dividing up the space. If 1d, can be integer. If 2d should be a list, defined as [number of x_bins, number of y_bins]. minimum : float, optional The minimum required occupancy. If defined, any values below this are set to zero. normalize : bool, optional, default: False Whether to normalize occupancy to sum to 1. set_nan : bool, optional, default: False Whether to set zero occupancy locations as NaN.
Returns ------- occupancy : 1d or 2d array Computed occupancy across the space. For 2d, has shape [n_y_bins, n_x_bins] (see notes in `compute_occupancy`). """
bins = check_bin_definition(bins)
# Group position samples into spatial bins, summing total time spent there groupby = sorted([el for el in list(bindf.columns) if 'bin' in el]) bingroup = bindf.groupby(groupby)['time'].sum() # This standardizes any unobserved values to be zero # This can otherwise vary by code version (sometimes being NaN instead) bingroup = bingroup.fillna(0)
# Extract and re-organize occupancy into array > occupancy = bingroup.values.reshape(*bins) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E ValueError: cannot reshape array of size 3 into shape (2,3)
spiketools/spatial/occupancy.py:419: ValueError

testcase.name: test_compute_trial_occupancy

failure.message: ValueError: cannot reshape array of size 3 into shape (2,3)

testcase.failure:
def test_compute_trial_occupancy():
# Test 1d case bins = 3 position = np.array([1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 9, 10, 2, 4, 6, 7, 8, 9]) timestamps = np.linspace(0, 60, len(position)) start_times = [0, 31] stop_times = [30, 60] trial_occupancy = compute_trial_occupancy( position, timestamps, bins, start_times, stop_times) expected1 = np.array([[15., 10., 5.], [10., 5., 10.]]) assert isinstance(trial_occupancy, np.ndarray) assert trial_occupancy.shape == (len(start_times), bins) assert np.array_equal(trial_occupancy, expected1)
# Test 2d case bins = [2, 3] position = np.array([[1, 2, 3, 4, 4.5, 5, 2, 2.5, 3.5, 4, 4.5, 5, 5.5], [6, 7, 8, 8.5, 9.5, 10, 6, 6.5, 7, 8, 8.5, 9.5, 10]]) timestamps = np.linspace(0, 60, position.shape[1]) start_times = [0, 31] stop_times = [30, 60] > trial_occupancy = compute_trial_occupancy( position, timestamps, bins, start_times, stop_times)
spiketools/tests/spatial/test_occupancy.py:262: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ spiketools/spatial/occupancy.py:587: in compute_trial_occupancy trial_occupancy[ind, :] = compute_occupancy(\ spiketools/spatial/occupancy.py:503: in compute_occupancy occupancy = compute_occupancy_df(df, bins, minimum, normalize, set_nan) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
bindf = time xbin ybin 0 5.0 0 0 1 5.0 0 0 2 5.0 1 1 3 5.0 1 1 4 5.0 1 2 5 5.0 1 2 6 0.0 0 0 bins = [2, 3], minimum = None, normalize = False, set_nan = False
def compute_occupancy_df(bindf, bins, minimum=None, normalize=False, set_nan=False): """Compute the bin occupancy from bin-position dataframe.
Parameters ---------- bindf : pd.DataFrame Dataframe representation of position bin information. bins : int or list of [int, int] The bin definition for dividing up the space. If 1d, can be integer. If 2d should be a list, defined as [number of x_bins, number of y_bins]. minimum : float, optional The minimum required occupancy. If defined, any values below this are set to zero. normalize : bool, optional, default: False Whether to normalize occupancy to sum to 1. set_nan : bool, optional, default: False Whether to set zero occupancy locations as NaN.
Returns ------- occupancy : 1d or 2d array Computed occupancy across the space. For 2d, has shape [n_y_bins, n_x_bins] (see notes in `compute_occupancy`). """
bins = check_bin_definition(bins)
# Group position samples into spatial bins, summing total time spent there groupby = sorted([el for el in list(bindf.columns) if 'bin' in el]) bingroup = bindf.groupby(groupby)['time'].sum() # This standardizes any unobserved values to be zero # This can otherwise vary by code version (sometimes being NaN instead) bingroup = bingroup.fillna(0)
# Extract and re-organize occupancy into array > occupancy = bingroup.values.reshape(*bins) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E ValueError: cannot reshape array of size 3 into shape (2,3)
spiketools/spatial/occupancy.py:419: ValueError

testcase.name: test_compute_trial_place_bins

failure.message: ValueError: cannot reshape array of size 1 into shape (2,3)

testcase.failure:
def test_compute_trial_place_bins():
spikes = np.array([0.1, 0.65, 1.1, 1.6, 1.9, 2.4, 2.9, 3.3, 3.77, 4.4]) position = np.array([[0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 1.5, 1.5, 1.5, 1.5, 1.5, 1.5], [0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 1.5, 1.5, 2.5, 2.5, 2.5, 2.5]]) timestamps = np.arange(0, 5.5, 0.5) start_times = np.array([0, 2.01]) stop_times = np.array([2.01, 4.51]) bins = [2, 3]
place_bins_trial = compute_trial_place_bins(spikes, position, timestamps, bins, start_times, stop_times) assert isinstance(place_bins_trial, np.ndarray) assert np.array_equal(place_bins_trial.shape, np.array([len(start_times), bins[1], bins[0]])) expected = np.array([[[0, 0], [0, 5], [0, 0]], [[0, 2], [0, 0], [0, 3]]]) assert np.array_equal(place_bins_trial, expected)
# Check with speed dropping speed = np.array([0, 1, 1, 1, 0, 1, 1, 0, 1, 1]) min_speed = 0.5 place_bins_trial = compute_trial_place_bins(spikes, position, timestamps, bins, start_times, stop_times, speed=speed, min_speed=min_speed) expected = np.array([[[0, 0], [0, 3], [0, 0]], [[0, 2], [0, 0], [0, 2]]]) assert np.array_equal(place_bins_trial, expected)
# Check with occupancy normalization > trial_occupancy = compute_trial_occupancy(position, timestamps, bins, start_times, stop_times) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
spiketools/tests/spatial/test_place.py:63: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ spiketools/spatial/occupancy.py:587: in compute_trial_occupancy trial_occupancy[ind, :] = compute_occupancy(\ spiketools/spatial/occupancy.py:503: in compute_occupancy occupancy = compute_occupancy_df(df, bins, minimum, normalize, set_nan) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
bindf = time xbin ybin 0 0.5 1 1 1 0.5 1 1 2 0.5 1 1 3 0.5 1 1 4 0.0 1 1 bins = [2, 3], minimum = None, normalize = False, set_nan = False
def compute_occupancy_df(bindf, bins, minimum=None, normalize=False, set_nan=False): """Compute the bin occupancy from bin-position dataframe.
Parameters ---------- bindf : pd.DataFrame Dataframe representation of position bin information. bins : int or list of [int, int] The bin definition for dividing up the space. If 1d, can be integer. If 2d should be a list, defined as [number of x_bins, number of y_bins]. minimum : float, optional The minimum required occupancy. If defined, any values below this are set to zero. normalize : bool, optional, default: False Whether to normalize occupancy to sum to 1. set_nan : bool, optional, default: False Whether to set zero occupancy locations as NaN.
Returns ------- occupancy : 1d or 2d array Computed occupancy across the space. For 2d, has shape [n_y_bins, n_x_bins] (see notes in `compute_occupancy`). """
bins = check_bin_definition(bins)
# Group position samples into spatial bins, summing total time spent there groupby = sorted([el for el in list(bindf.columns) if 'bin' in el]) bingroup = bindf.groupby(groupby)['time'].sum() # This standardizes any unobserved values to be zero # This can otherwise vary by code version (sometimes being NaN instead) bingroup = bingroup.fillna(0)
# Extract and re-organize occupancy into array > occupancy = bingroup.values.reshape(*bins) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E ValueError: cannot reshape array of size 1 into shape (2,3)
spiketools/spatial/occupancy.py:419: ValueError

testcase.name: test_fit_anova

failure.message: ImportError: Optional dependency statsmodels is required for this functionality.
